item.title: Self Driving Car Nanodegree - Project 4: Advanced Lane Finding
item.description: Utilized OpenCV to create a robust image processing pipeline for identifying the highway lane in an image and video stream

item.title: Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng
item.description: Detect highway lane lines from a video stream. Use OpenCV image analysis techniques to identify lines, including Hough transforms and Canny edge detection.

item.title: Self Driving Car Nanodegree - Project 1: Detect Lane Lines
item.description: Utilized OpenCV and image analysis algorithms including Hough Transforms and Canny edge detection for identifying the highway lane in an image and video stream.

item.title: Self Driving Car Nanodegree - Project 2: Traffic Sign Classifier
item.description: Utilized TensorFlow deep learning framework and OpenCV to train a classifier for the traffic sign dataset.

item.title: Deep Learning Nanodegree Foundation (Udacity)
item.description: The goal for this project was to classify images from hotels into one of 8 classes. I implemented a Convolutional Neural Network using “keras” library (Deep Learning library for Theano and TensorFlow) to solve the classification problem including more than 80000 training examples with somewhat noisy ground truth labels and more than 20000 unlabeled testing images.

item.title: Deep Learning by Google Brain (Udacity)
item.description: Implemented the deep learning techniques using Google Tensorflow that cover deep neural networks with a fully connected network using SGD and ReLUs; Regularization with a multi-layer neural network using ReLUs, L2-regularization, and dropout, to prevent overfitting; Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with learning rate decay and dropout; and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) for text and sequences with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks.

item.title: Machine Learning by Stanford University
item.description: #The goal for this project was to classify images from hotels into one of 8 classes. I implemented a Convolutional Neural Network using “keras” library (Deep Learning library for Theano and TensorFlow) to solve the classification problem including more than 80000 training examples with somewhat noisy ground truth labels and more than 20000 unlabeled testing images.

item.title: Deep Learning (Class project)
item.description: The goal for this project was to classify images from hotels into one of 8 classes. I implemented a Convolutional Neural Network using “keras” library to solve the classification problem including more than 80000 training examples with somewhat noisy ground truth labels and more than 20000 unlabeled testing images.
